summary: Israel launched a large‑scale air attack on the Lebanese city of Sour and continued strikes in southern Lebanon. Israeli forces also hit Hamas and Palestinian Islamic Jihad targets in Gaza, destroying a naval police headquarters and killing two PIJ commanders. Iran reported the death of two air‑defence personnel in Israeli strikes and claimed to have downed a U.S. Apache helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz.
key_points: Israel bombed the Lebanese port city of Sour from early morning, described as a massive attack (Source: @rodast_omiddana/72967).
The Israeli Air Force struck Hamas's naval police headquarters in Khan Younis, killing commander Ismail al‑Lahham and destroying three arms caches (Source: @manniefabian/58521).
Two Palestinian Islamic Jihad commanders, Iyad Nofal and Ahmed Marouf, were killed in an Israeli strike in the southern Gaza Strip (Source: @manniefabian/58523).

Givati Brigade troops operating north of the Litani River in Zawtar al‑Sharqiyah and Zawtar al‑Gharbiya seized firearms, anti‑tank missiles, drones and other gear from civilian buildings (Source: @manniefabian/58529).
Iran announced that two of its air‑defence personnel, Bahman Hosseini and Ali‑Raza Abiri, were killed in Israeli attacks (Source: @New_security8200/201488).
Iranian officials claimed Iranian forces shot down a U.S. Apache helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz, with both crew members unharmed (Source: @VahidOnline/76102).
U.S. sources confirmed a U.S. Army Apache helicopter went down near the Strait of Hormuz, cause under investigation (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3656).

sections.executive_summary: Israel launched a massive air assault on the Lebanese city of Sour, extending its strike campaign into southern Lebanon. Simultaneously, Israeli forces hit high‑value Hamas and PIJ targets in Gaza, killing senior commanders and destroying weapons caches. Iran reported casualties from Israeli strikes and claimed to have downed a U.S. Apache helicopter, raising regional tension.

points: Two Iranian air‑defence officers, Bahman Hosseini and Ali‑Raza Abiri, were reported killed in Israeli strikes, indicating direct Israeli action against Iranian personnel in the region (Source: @New_security8200/201488).

Iranian officials asserted that Iranian forces shot down an advanced U.S. Apache helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz, with both pilots surviving, suggesting heightened anti‑U.S. activity in the Gulf (Source: @VahidOnline/76102).
Jet activity was observed over western Tehran, reflecting increased Iranian air‑defence alertness amid regional hostilities (Source: @shin_persian/6779).
Iranian statements emphasized readiness to respond to perceived threats, aligning with broader regional posturing (Source: @kianmeli1/87814).

The Israeli army declared full readiness to resume conflict with Tehran, maintaining all defensive and offensive systems on alert and claiming to have intercepted threats before striking Iran (Source: @kianmeli1/87814).

IDF chief of staff described recent attacks on Iran as preparation for a larger operation and pledged continued strikes against Hezbollah to protect northern Israeli communities (Source: @New_security8200/201490).
IDF reported engaging a terrorist near Marjaliyat in southern Lebanon, eliminating the threat without Israeli casualties (Source: @inon_yttach/8796).
IDF forces on Ramim Ridge repelled an attack, killing a terrorist and continuing aerial patrols in the area (Source: @amitsegal/62003).
Israeli airstrikes in the Khan Younis area destroyed Hamas's naval police headquarters, killed commander Ismail al‑Lahham, and eliminated three weapons caches (Source: @manniefabian/58521).
Two senior Palestinian Islamic Jihad operatives, Iyad Nofal and Ahmed Marouf, were killed in a targeted strike in the southern Gaza Strip (Source: @manniefabian/58523).
Israel conducted a large‑scale bombing of the Lebanese port city of Sour, described as a “massive attack” (Source: @rodast_omiddana/72967).

IDF forces of the Givati Brigade operating north of the Litani River in Zawtar al‑Sharqiyah and Zawtar al‑Gharbiya seized firearms, anti‑tank missiles, drones and other military equipment hidden in civilian structures (Source: @manniefabian/58529).
A recent IDF report confirmed the killing of a terrorist approaching the settlement of Marjaliyat in southern Lebanon (Source: @inon_yttach/8796).
A U.S. Army Apache helicopter went down near the Strait of Hormuz; investigations are ongoing to determine whether it was shot down or suffered a mechanical failure (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3656).

Iran claimed responsibility for downing the same Apache helicopter, stating the incident occurred during a patrol over the Strait of Hormuz and that both crew members were unharmed (Source: @VahidOnline/76102).

key_developments: Israel launched a massive air attack on the Lebanese city of Sour, marking a significant escalation on the northern front (Source: @rodast_omiddana/72967).
The Israeli Air Force destroyed Hamas's naval police headquarters in Khan Younis, killing commander Ismail al‑Lahham and eliminating three weapons caches (Source: @manniefabian/58521).
Two senior Palestinian Islamic Jihad commanders, Iyad Nofal and Ahmed Marouf, were killed in an Israeli strike in southern Gaza (Source: @manniefabian/58523).

Givati Brigade troops seized firearms, anti‑tank missiles, drones and other gear from civilian buildings north of the Litani River in Zawtar al‑Sharqiyah and Zawtar al‑Gharbiya (Source: @manniefabian/58529).
Iran reported the death of two air‑defence personnel, Bahman Hosseini and Ali‑Raza Abiri, in Israeli strikes (Source: @New_security8200/201488).
Iranian officials claimed Iranian forces shot down a U.S. Apache helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz, with both pilots surviving (Source: @VahidOnline/76102).
U.S. sources confirmed a U.S. Army Apache helicopter went down near the Strait of Hormuz, cause under investigation (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3656).
sections.threat_assessment: The coordinated Israeli strikes in Lebanon and Gaza demonstrate an expanding operational tempo aimed at degrading both Hezbollah and Hamas capabilities. Iran's reported casualties and its claim of downing a U.S. Apache indicate a willingness to engage directly with regional adversaries, raising the risk of broader confrontation. The downing of a U.S. helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz adds a volatile element to Gulf security, potentially inviting retaliatory actions from Iran or proxy forces.
sections.regional_response: Lebanese authorities have not issued a public response, but the intensity of Israeli bombardment in Sour may provoke Hezbollah retaliation. Iran's rhetoric and alleged anti‑U.S. action suggest it may increase support for proxy groups in the region. The United States is likely to investigate the Apache incident while maintaining a heightened presence in the Gulf to deter further escalation.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Israeli air operations over southern Lebanon should focus on target patterns and potential Hezbollah counter‑strikes. Surveillance of Iranian air‑defence deployments is needed after the reported loss of two personnel. The Gulf corridor around the Strait of Hormuz requires increased maritime and aerial reconnaissance to assess Iranian anti‑air capabilities following the helicopter incident.
